judgment.summary: Zscaler exhibits strong revenue growth and healthy free cash flow, but is not yet profitable on a GAAP basis and trades at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: For the quarter ending 2026-01-31, Zscaler reported EPS of $1.01, beating the estimate of $0.90 by 12.6%. The next earnings date is scheduled for 2026-05-26.
positives: Revenue grew 25.9% year-over-year, indicating strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $1.02B TTM demonstrates robust cash generation capabilities.
Gross margin of 76.6% is very healthy, suggesting efficient cost management on goods sold.
The current ratio of 1.90 and quick ratio of 1.75 indicate solid short-term liquidity.
Analysts have a consensus 'buy' rating with a mean target price of $224.27, suggesting optimism.
negatives: Operating margin of -6.2% and profit margin of -2.3% show the company is not currently profitable on an operating basis.
Return on equity of -3.6% and return on assets of -1.4% highlight negative profitability metrics.
Forward P/E of 38.26x and PEG ratio of 1.88x suggest a relatively high valuation, priced for future growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 85.00x is notable, though offset by substantial cash reserves.

news.paragraph: Zscaler shares have experienced a significant rebound, with recent headlines highlighting its strategic focus on enterprise AI security through initiatives like Project AI Guardian and partnerships with Google Cloud and global system integrators. This renewed investor interest, coupled with a general software sector recovery, suggests positive momentum. Traders may want to consider the upcoming May 26th date mentioned in some reports as a potential point of focus.
